output.market_context: Expansion regime with stable rates (Fed funds 3.63%, 10Y at 4.45%), moderating inflation, and a risk-on tape catalyzed by a US-Iran peace deal driving futures higher and oil sharply lower. News flow is uniformly bullish across mega-cap tech, semis, financials and broad indices; sentiment scout is degraded (uniformly neutral) so technical + news + insider carry the weight.
themes: Risk-on rotation on US-Iran peace deal lifting cyclicals and small caps
Oil down hard - XLE weak, materials/transports beneficiary
Mag-7 dispersion: META/AAPL stable, NVDA/AMZN/MSFT technically weak
Insider selling cluster in AMD/MSFT/TSLA/WMT - caution flag on chase

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Memory super-cycle breakout with bull MA stack, +40% 1M momentum, and high-buzz positive news flow.
ranked_candidates.narrative: MU is the cleanest technical setup in the universe: bull stack, 20D breakout, RSI 66 not yet extreme, and news polarity 0.95 across 6 sources tied to memory/AI capex. Macro expansion regime favors semis. No insider data and degraded sentiment scout cap conviction slightly, but cross-signal alignment is strong.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: AI inference momentum + $900B valuation narrative, but Lisa Su cluster-selling ~$57M is a notable counter-signal.
ranked_candidates.narrative: Strongest news polarity in the universe (0.95) with bull MA stack and 20D breakout post-earnings beat. The offset is real: CEO Lisa Su sold 27 lots totaling ~$57M last week. Net: still a buy candidate given the breakout, but score is haircut for insider distribution into strength.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Dow breakout on US-Iran peace deal with highest news polarity in universe (0.99).
ranked_candidates.narrative: Bull MA stack, 20D breakout, RSI 63.6, and event-driven catalyst (peace deal) lifting Dow components. ETF so no insider/earnings; macro expansion supports broad index.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Small-cap breakout with bull stack and easing rates supports the risk-on rotation.
ranked_candidates.narrative: Strong technical (20D breakout, bull MA, RSI 61.5) and 10Y yield drop to 4.45% are tailwinds for small caps. News polarity is negative (-0.78) on sticky-inflation worry, which is the main drag. Net positive on technicals.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Financials breakout in expansion regime with positive sector news.
ranked_candidates.narrative: Bull MA stack, RSI 67.5 (warm but not extreme), 20D breakout, and financials are a favored sector in the macro regime. News flow constructive. Watch RSI.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Tech sector ETF riding AI-capex narrative with bull MA stack.
ranked_candidates.narrative: Bull stack, RSI 61.6, +6.8% 1M momentum, high-polarity news (0.87) on AI ETF flows. Macro regime favors tech. No breakout yet but trend intact.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Industrials breakout in expansion regime; favored sector with lower-oil tailwind.
ranked_candidates.narrative: Bull MA stack, 20D breakout, RSI 61.2. Industrials are macro-favored and benefit from oil-down/peace-deal narrative. News polarity slightly negative but technicals dominate.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Nasdaq strength with bull stack and high-polarity AI-themed news flow.
ranked_candidates.narrative: Bull MA, RSI 62.4, news polarity 0.90 across air-taxi/AI security themes. Macro tilt supports tech. No 20D breakout yet but trend healthy.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Broad market trend intact with high-polarity news and peace-deal catalyst.
ranked_candidates.narrative: Bull MA stack, RSI 60.5, polarity 0.96. Cleanest passive expression of the risk-on tape.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Materials breakout on oil-down/peace-deal infrastructure tailwind.
ranked_candidates.narrative: 20D breakout, +5.7% 1W momentum, RSI 60.4, news polarity 0.90 explicitly citing materials beneficiary of oil drop. MA stack is mixed which caps the score.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Bull-stack breakout with Cramer/JPM upgrades; offset by RSI 70 and healthcare sector weakness.
ranked_candidates.narrative: Strong earnings beat (+9.5% surprise), 20D breakout, RSI 70 (overbought), positive news polarity 0.80, JPM PT raise. Healthcare disfavored in macro regime and XLV news polarity is negative. Quality name but extended.

ranked_candidates.narrative: 20D breakout, RSI 65, earnings beat. News headlines lean cautious citing Buffett's correction warnings, dampening conviction. Solid hold but not a high-octane buy.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Defensive bid but macro-disfavored.
ranked_candidates.narrative: Bull MA stack, RSI 58.5, polarity 0.48. Staples are disfavored in expansion regime, so this is more of a hedge than a thesis.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Steady compounder in favored financials sector with high-polarity news.
ranked_candidates.narrative: Bull MA, RSI 52.8 (room to run), earnings beat, polarity 0.95 on digital payments/World Cup themes. Lacks a momentum catalyst.

ranked_candidates.narrative: Bull MA, RSI 53.8, polarity 0.53. Consumer sentiment indicator weakened (49.8 vs 53.3) — slight headwind. Decent but not exceptional.

ranked_candidates.narrative: Mixed MA, RSI 51, polarity -0.33 with TSMC capacity crunch headlines. Wait for trend repair.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Energy crushed by US-Iran peace deal driving oil down ~5.7% weekly.
ranked_candidates.narrative: RSI 41, -4.2% 1W, mixed MA. News polarity is mechanically positive due to volume but the actual catalyst is bearish for energy. Avoid.

ranked_candidates.narrative: Below MAs, -9% 1M, EVP sold $1.4M, staples macro-disfavored. Quality but wait.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: -29.5% 1W collapse despite huge earnings beat and bullish news — falling knife.
ranked_candidates.narrative: Catastrophic 1W move (-29.5%) suggests material adverse news not captured. News polarity is high but headlines mix in unrelated airline content. Avoid until basing.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Earnings miss, CFO insider sale, SpaceX overshadowing — news polarity is event-driven not Tesla-driven.
ranked_candidates.narrative: Mixed MA, RSI 50, -7.5% 1M, recent -36% earnings surprise miss, CFO sold ~$1M. News polarity 0.98 but headlines are dominated by SpaceX > Tesla valuation narrative, which is actually bearish for TSLA. Avoid.

lessons_for_pm: Highest-conviction semis with technical+earnings+news triple-alignment (MU +27%, NVDA winners) are our edge — keep upsizing the cleanest cross-signal name, but enforce a trim trigger when RSI prints >78 (MU at RSI 80 was held, then gave back -8.3%).

News polarity ≥80 alone is NOT a thesis. XLE (-5.5%), BRK.B (-4.1%), GOOGL (-4.8%), AVGO (-14.3%), MSFT (-11.8%) all opened with news ≥80 but middling technical/earnings and all lost. Require ≥2 of {technical ≥75, earnings beat, macro_fit ≥65} before sizing >5%.

Insider-sell flags >$30M have been near-perfectly predictive: MU CEO $38M → -8.3% week; META/WMT/AMD avoidances all correct. Hard rule: any single-name insider sale >$30M = max 3% or pass, regardless of other factors.
Conviction='high' on a single dominant factor has burned us — MSFT high-conviction at 9% on technical=82 lost 11.8% in one week. Reserve 'high' for ≥3 factors >70 with zero insider/macro red flags.

TLH execution is working ($23 tax savings captured), but immediate redeployment into the next flagged name (AVGO insider, AMD high-vol post-MSFT close) repeats the mistake. Park TLH proceeds in cash 1 week before redeploying.
Medium-conviction picks have an 11% win rate and -3.6% avg return — they are diluting alpha. Either upgrade thesis to high or pass; stop the 5-6% 'starter' positions on mediocre setups.
output.summary: 4 weeks tracked, cumulative -0.87% vs SPY -1.08% (+20bps alpha), but win rate is only 25% weekly and 21% per-pick — the book is being carried by one outlier (MU +27%) while medium-conviction news-led opens (XLE, BRK.B, GOOGL, AVGO, MSFT) consistently lose 4-14%. High-conviction picks win 40% with +7.4% avg; medium picks win 11% with -3.6% avg.
watch_for: Overbought semis sleeve (NVDA/SMCI/MU/AMD) — concentration risk if AI capex narrative cracks; consider partial trim on any name with RSI >78 + insider activity.
Repeating the 'news polarity ≥85 = buy' pattern. Five of five such opens have lost. Demand technical/earnings confirmation this week.
Redeployed TLH cash chasing into the next flagged name within the same week — break the reflex; let cash sit if no clean cross-signal candidate exists.
what_missed: News-polarity-led opens without technical/earnings confirmation: XLE (-5.5%), BRK.B (-4.1%), GOOGL (-4.8%), AVGO (-14.3%), MSFT (-11.8%) — 5 of 5 lost, avg -7.7%.
Sizing MSFT at 9% high-conviction on a single dominant factor (technical=82) — lost 11.8% in one week, the largest single drag in the book.
Not trimming MU when RSI hit 80 the week before the CEO $38M sale; we waited a week and gave back 8.3% before halving.
Medium-conviction bucket overall: 9 picks, 11% win rate, -3.6% avg — diluting alpha from the high-conviction sleeve.
what_worked: MU OPEN at 5% on technical(82)+news(94)+earnings cross-signal → +27% return, best pick in the book and the entire reason cumulative alpha is positive.
Reflection-driven 'upsize the winner' rule applied to UNH (+6%) and NVDA (+6%) — the only ADDs in the book and both went on to win in their first week.
Insider-flag avoidances were 100% correct: META, WMT, TSLA, AAPL (week 1) all flagged and passed — would have been losers.
TLH discipline on broken theses (AMZN, GOOGL, BRK.B, XLE, MSFT) — captured $23 in tax savings and prevented bigger drawdowns from cumulative losers.

lessons_for_pm: Highest-conviction semis with technical+earnings+news triple-alignment (MU +27%, NVDA winners) are our edge — keep upsizing the cleanest cross-signal name, but enforce a trim trigger when RSI prints >78 (MU at RSI 80 was held, then gave back -8.3%).

News polarity ≥80 alone is NOT a thesis. XLE (-5.5%), BRK.B (-4.1%), GOOGL (-4.8%), AVGO (-14.3%), MSFT (-11.8%) all opened with news ≥80 but middling technical/earnings and all lost. Require ≥2 of {technical ≥75, earnings beat, macro_fit ≥65} before sizing >5%.

Insider-sell flags >$30M have been near-perfectly predictive: MU CEO $38M → -8.3% week; META/WMT/AMD avoidances all correct. Hard rule: any single-name insider sale >$30M = max 3% or pass, regardless of other factors.
Conviction='high' on a single dominant factor has burned us — MSFT high-conviction at 9% on technical=82 lost 11.8% in one week. Reserve 'high' for ≥3 factors >70 with zero insider/macro red flags.

TLH execution is working ($23 tax savings captured), but immediate redeployment into the next flagged name (AVGO insider, AMD high-vol post-MSFT close) repeats the mistake. Park TLH proceeds in cash 1 week before redeploying.
Medium-conviction picks have an 11% win rate and -3.6% avg return — they are diluting alpha. Either upgrade thesis to high or pass; stop the 5-6% 'starter' positions on mediocre setups.
output.summary: 4 weeks tracked, cumulative -0.87% vs SPY -1.08% (+20bps alpha), but win rate is only 25% weekly and 21% per-pick — the book is being carried by one outlier (MU +27%) while medium-conviction news-led opens (XLE, BRK.B, GOOGL, AVGO, MSFT) consistently lose 4-14%. High-conviction picks win 40% with +7.4% avg; medium picks win 11% with -3.6% avg.
watch_for: Overbought semis sleeve (NVDA/SMCI/MU/AMD) — concentration risk if AI capex narrative cracks; consider partial trim on any name with RSI >78 + insider activity.
Repeating the 'news polarity ≥85 = buy' pattern. Five of five such opens have lost. Demand technical/earnings confirmation this week.
Redeployed TLH cash chasing into the next flagged name within the same week — break the reflex; let cash sit if no clean cross-signal candidate exists.
what_missed: News-polarity-led opens without technical/earnings confirmation: XLE (-5.5%), BRK.B (-4.1%), GOOGL (-4.8%), AVGO (-14.3%), MSFT (-11.8%) — 5 of 5 lost, avg -7.7%.
Sizing MSFT at 9% high-conviction on a single dominant factor (technical=82) — lost 11.8% in one week, the largest single drag in the book.
Not trimming MU when RSI hit 80 the week before the CEO $38M sale; we waited a week and gave back 8.3% before halving.
Medium-conviction bucket overall: 9 picks, 11% win rate, -3.6% avg — diluting alpha from the high-conviction sleeve.

Reflection-driven 'upsize the winner' rule applied to UNH (+6%) and NVDA (+6%) — the only ADDs in the book and both went on to win in their first week.
Insider-flag avoidances were 100% correct: META, WMT, TSLA, AAPL (week 1) all flagged and passed — would have been losers.

decisions.thesis: Cleanest cross-signal name: technical 85, news 95, macro fit 75, bull stack 20D breakout — upsizing the winner per reflection lesson #1.
Lisa Su cluster sale ~$57M is borderline >$30M hard-rule territory; technical breakout supports holding but no add.
Highest news polarity (0.99), bull stack 20D breakout, peace-deal catalyst — broad-index expression of the risk-on tape.
Technicals strong but news polarity negative on sticky-inflation worry — conflicting signal, pass.
Financials breakout in expansion regime, RSI 67 not extreme, sector tailwind — diversifier away from semis.
Already heavy in single-name tech (NVDA/AMD/AVGO/MU/SMCI/AAPL) — adding XLK would over-concentrate sector.
Industrials 20D breakout, macro-favored, beneficiary of oil-down/peace-deal narrative.
No 20D breakout yet and existing tech sleeve already covers the theme.
DIA chosen as the broad-index expression; SPY would duplicate exposure.
Mixed MA stack caps conviction; XLI captures cyclical-rotation theme more cleanly.
RSI 70 + macro mismatch (healthcare disfavored) + extended after JPM upgrade — bank gains on extension per reflection lesson #1.
Wash-sale blocked through 2026-06-18 — hard block.
Staples macro-disfavored in expansion regime, no thesis.
Solid compounder but lacks momentum catalyst; XLF captures financials exposure more efficiently.
Consumer sentiment weakening (49.8 vs 53.3) — pass.
Mixed MA stack; XLF provides cleaner sector exposure.
Negative news polarity, not macro-favored.
Bear MA stack + RSI 46 — sentiment-only setup, reflection lesson #2 says skip.
Utilities explicitly macro-disfavored.
Bear stack and macro mismatch.
Sentiment-only flag, mixed technicals, -1.8% 1W; TLH-eligible with ~$10 tax savings — exit and park cash per reflection lesson #5.
Consolidating with earnings beat + news 96; not adding into stalled price action but thesis intact.
Below 20D/50D, falling-knife risk despite earnings beat.
Negative news polarity and weak trend — wait for repair.
Insider selling + bearish technicals + lawsuit overhang — explicit reflection-lesson skip.
TLH harvest — $88 unrealized loss / $30 tax savings, RSI 45, -10.3% 1M, post-earnings drift; park proceeds in cash per reflection lesson #5.
Energy crushed by peace deal / oil down — explicit macro mismatch.
Bear stack + insider sale + staples disfavored.
-29.5% 1W collapse signals adverse news not captured; TLH-eligible ($22 tax savings), reflection-lesson cut on falling-knife semis.
Earnings miss + CFO sale + SpaceX overshadowing — avoid.

output.weekly_thesis: Concentrate into the cleanest cross-signal name (MU), diversify the risk-on tape via DIA/XLF/XLI ETFs, and harvest losses on broken theses (AVGO, SMCI, AAPL) while trimming extended UNH.
output.narrative: The tape is risk-on with clean technical breakouts in semis and broad indices, but reflection lessons mandate discipline: avoid news-polarity-only chases, hard-cap on insider-sell flags, and don't repeat the immediate-redeploy mistake. I'm upsizing MU as our highest-conviction cross-signal winner, adding ETF diversification via DIA/XLF/XLI to broaden the risk-on expression without piling more into single-name semis, and harvesting losses on AVGO (TLH $30 savings), SMCI (broken thesis after -29.5% week), and AAPL (sentiment-only, no trend). UNH gets trimmed 50% to bank the breakout gains given RSI 70 + macro mismatch. Resulting cash buffer (~22%) sits within operating range and respects the "let TLH cash sit" rule by not redeploying every dollar this week.

overrides.reason: RSI 70 + healthcare macro-mismatch + reflection lesson #1 (trim extended winners) — bank gains on the JPM-upgrade pop.
TLH opportunity ($10 savings), no technical confirmation, sentiment-only flag — recycle into cash per lesson #5 rather than holding a mediocre setup.
-29.5% week signals unmodeled adverse news; TLH-eligible — exit decisively rather than average down a broken thesis.

open_picks.thesis: Healthcare laggard with strong news (90) and improving technicals (74) offers uncorrelated exposure to AI/tech beta.
Earnings Wed is binary but technical setup (74) and AI capex narrative intact; sized small to respect event risk on first deployment.
#2 composite with technical 82 + news 94 — memory cycle riding AI capex; half-cap size due to high_vol flag.
AI capex super-cycle dominant theme, semis leading, NVDA earnings resolved favorably — reflection lesson explicitly says size the highest-conviction setup meaningfully.
Strong momentum, bull MA stack, earnings beat, AI tailwind; insider sell caps size but cross-signals justify a position.
Cleanest cross-signal name: bull MA stack, breakout, +8.9% 1M, strong news polarity, earnings tailwind; upsize the winner per reflection lesson.
